code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test27-NOV-2022Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephropathy and metabolic derangements. Low HbA1c with high urine glucose may suggest recent onset or fluctuating hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and assessment of glycemic status.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
